Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»When is Chad le Clos in action at the Paris Olympics?

Here is when you can first catch famed Olympian swimmer Chad le Clos in action at the Paris Games this week.

Chad le Clos enters the Paris Olympics as an outside medal hope after struggling with form and an injury concern recently, but based on his history, there will always be great expectation that follow in his wake.

Le Clos introduced himself to the world at the 2012 Olympic Games in London where he beat American icon Michael Phelps in the 200m butterfly, before also claiming the 100m fly silver medal.

He followed that up with 200m free and 100m butterfly silvers in Rio, but there was great disappointment when he failed to reach the expected standards at the Tokyo Oympics three years ago.

Le Clos will only be taking part in the 100m butterfly event this year, and hewill be in action on Friday, 2 August, in heat two – which will get under way around11am (SA time).

The 32-year-old multi-Olympic star toldReutersrecently that he is suffering from a muscle injury heading into his fourth Olympic Games.

“I had a bit of an incident,” he said of a training mishap. “I had a small tear in my upper trap, this is 16 days ago, but everything is good. I’m lucky to have amazing doctors and physios in the national team.

“We’re doing everything we can. I’ve had a great, great camp and I’m in fantastic shape. May the best three guys win on the day.”

“I’m the third oldest male swimmer at these Games, so in those terms I’m something of a pensioner,” Le Clos toldTeam South Africa.

“And in spite of picking up a shoulder tear in training, I’ve come to Paris believing I have a chance. I’ve raced with injuries before.

“I think that the semi-final will be the harder race, because there will be 16 swimmers going for a place in the final. I’m hoping to get there and once I do I’ll be chasing another dream. I’m a warrior, a born racer.

“Yes, I’ve had an injury the last two weeks. I went to the medical team as soon as I arrived from Montpellier and had it checked out. The first thing I did after that was head for the dining hall!

“I’m here to enjoy the experience, even if I don’t think it’s my last, and give of my best.”

South Africa’s most decorated Olympian of all time with four medals, Chad Le Closscored one of the sensations of London 2012, defeating the great Michael Phelps in the 200m butterfly.

Four years later, he shared silver with the American great in a stunning 100m butterfly final in Rio.

title: When is Chad le Clos in action at the Paris Olympics?
author: Craig Lewis
description: Here is when you can first catch famed Olympian swimmer Chad le Clos in action at the Paris Games this week.
